entry.title: Learning APIM Series (4) - Enabling API activity diagnostics logs with APIM
entry.content:
# Enabling API activity diagnostics logs with APIM

# 1. Enable diagnostics logs on APIM

# (1) Enable diagnostics logs on APIM

Ensure you have enabled diagnostics logs on APIM resource.

# 2. Enable Azure Monitor logs to Information level on APIM

Enable Azure diagnostic logs to Information level and change payload to 8192 bytes.`

# 3. Review the logs on Log Analytics workspace

# Reference

  • [1]Tutorial: Monitor published APIs
  • [2]Enterprise Azure OpenAI

entry.title: Learning APIM Series (3) - Configuring multiple Azure OpenAI instances with APIM and managed identity
entry.content:
# Learning APIM Series (3) - Configuring multiple Azure OpenAI instances with APIM and managed identity

# 1. Enable APIM managed identity

# (1) Enable APIM managed identity

Ensure you have enabled APIM managed identity on APIM resource.

# 2. Prepare Azure OpenAI resource

# (1) Add role assignment to APIM managed identity

A. Add role assignment with Cognitive Services OpenAI User role

B. Assign to APIM managed identity

Note: You have to add role assignment on all Azure OpenAI resources

# (2) Add a deployment of Azure OpenAI resource on Azure OpenAI

Note: You have to add the same deployment name for Azure OpenAI resources

# 3. Configure APIM with Azure OpenAI backends

# (1) Add Azure OpenAI backends

# 4. Add Azure API with OpenAI

# (1) Add Azure API with OpenAI

A. Add OpenAPI with Add API on APIM API pane

# (2) Create from Open API specification

A. Select Full on Create from OpenAPI specification

B. Download the OpenAPI specification from the URL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/main/specification/cognitiveservices/data-plane/AzureOpenAI/inference/stable/2023-05-15/inference.json [1]

C. Modify the Server setting to the OpenAI endpoint url

D. Upload the json file to OpenAPI setting

E. Add openai as API URL suffix

F. Add Starter and Unlimited as products

# 5. Set the Inbound policy to add the managed identity token

# (1) Add the following policy to the Inbound policy [2]

# 6. Add Inbound policy for Azure OpenAI redundancy [3]

# (1) Add the following policy to the Inbound policy

# 7. Add retry Backend policy

# (1) Add the following policy to the Backend policy

# 8. Configure APIM subscription to accept api-key header and query parameter

# 9. Test the APIM in a VM with VNet integration

# (1) Get the subscription key from APIM

# (2) Add APIM fqdn to hosts file

# (3) Using curl to test the APIM

# Reference

  • [1]Azure OpenAI API 2023-05-15 Swagger spec
  • [2]Authenticate with managed identity
  • [3]api-management-policy-snippets-Back-end API redundancy.policy.xml

entry.title: Enable Custom Translator with VNet integration
entry.content:
# Enable Custom Translator with VNet integration

# 1. 建立新的 Translator 資源

# 1.1 請先建立一個 Public 的 Translator 資源，確認 Allow public access

# 1.2 同時建立 Private endpoint

# 2. 在 Custom Translator Portal 先將 Workspace 加入

# 2.1 建立 Workspace

# 2.2 取得 Workspace ID

# 3. 確認 VNet 中建立一個 VM 可以連線到 Private endpoint

# 4. 將 Custom Translator 啟用為 VNet integration

# 4.1 將 Firewalls and virtual networks 設定為 Selected networks and private endpoints, 並加入 #3 VM 所在的 subnet, 加入完成後，Customer Translator 便啟用了 VNet integration (全部完成設定後可以改為 Disabled)

參考: Enable Custom Translator through Azure Virtual Network

# 5. 登入 VM 開啟 Command Prompt, 執行以下的命令，將 workspace 重新建立為 VNet integration

# 5.1 建立 workspace, 其中 workspace 名稱需要與 #2.1 建立的名稱相同

// create workspace
curl.exe  -X POST "https://<resource-name>.cognitiveservices.azure.com/translator/customtranslator/api/texttranslator/v1.0/workspaces" --header "Content-Type: application/json" -H "Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key:<resource-key>" --data "{ 'Name': '<workspace-name>', 'Subscription': { 'SubscriptionKey': '<resource-key>', 'BillingRegionCode': '<billing-region-code>' } }"

# 5.2 取得支援的語言代碼

// get supported LanguagePairId code
curl.exe -X GET   "https://<resource-name>.cognitiveservices.azure.com/translator/customtranslator/api/texttranslator/v1.0/languages/supportedlanguagepairs" --header "Content-Type: application/json" -H "Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key:<resource-key>"

# 5.3 建立 project, 其中 workspace id 為 #2.2 取得的 workspace id, sourceLanguageCode 與 targetLanguageCode 可以參考 #5.2 取得支援的語言代碼

// get supported LanguagePairId code
curl.exe -X GET   "https://<resource-name>.cognitiveservices.azure.com/translator/customtranslator/api/texttranslator/v1.0/languages/supportedlanguagepairs" --header "Content-Type: application/json" -H "Ocp-Apim-Subscription-Key:<resource-key>"

# 5.4 在 VM 開啟 Custom Translator Portal, 確認已經可以看到 #5.3 建立的 project

entry.title: Install pip packages offline
entry.content:
# Install pip packages offline

# 1. Download pip packages on a machine with internet access

# (1) Download pip packages

# (2) Archive the pip packages

# (3) Copy the archive file to the target machine, extract and install it

entry.title: Create a local rpm repo for offline installation
entry.content:
# Create a local rpm repo for offline installation

# 1. Creating a local repo and download rpm packages on a machine with internet access

# (1) Install createrepo package

# (2) Download rpm packages

# (3) Create local repo

# (4) Archive the local repo files

# 2. Install offline rpm packages on a machine without internet access

# (1) Copy the local repo files to the target machine

# (2) Create a yum repo file

with the following content

# (3) Install rpm packages

entry.title: Gathering CycleCloud service JVM heap usage to Log Analytics
entry.content:
# Gathering CycleCloud service JVM heap usage to Log Analytics

# 1. 準備事項

CycleCloud Service 使用 Java 開發，因此在安裝 CycleCloud Service 時，會自動安裝 Java 執行環境。而 Java 執行環境的記憶體使用量，可以透過 JMX (Java Management Extensions) 來取得。而 Heap 使用的資訊，可以透過 jstat 命令來取得。因此我們需要先安裝一些必要的套件，才能夠將 CycleCloud 的 JVM heap usage 資訊傳送到 Log Analytics 中。

# (1) 安裝必要的套件

A. java-1.8.0-openjdk-devel 套件
sudo yum install java-1.8.0-openjdk-devel

B. Python 使用 Azure SDK 相關的套件

sudo pip3 install azure-identity
sudo pip3 install azure-keyvault-secrets
sudo pip3 install azure-monitor-query
sudo pip3 install azure-core

# (2) 準備相關的服務與權限

A. CycleCloud 需要啟用 Managed Identity
B. Log Analytics workspace 並取得 Workspace ID 與 Primary Key 儲存於 Key Vault 中
C. Key Vault 存放 Log Analytics workspace ID 與 Primary Key

# Scope Account Account Type Role or Permission Description
1 Key Vault CycleCloud VM Managed Identity Managed Identity Log Analytics Secrets Get 取得 Log Analytics 的 Workspace ID 與 Primary Key

# 2. 設定 CycleCloud Service JVM heap usage 收集到 Log Analytics

# (1) 登入 CycleCloud Server, 確認 jstat 可以正確取得 heap usage 資訊

jstat -gcutil <pid>

# (2) 登入 CycleCloud, 建立 Custom Script 目錄

mkdir ~/scripts

# (3) 建立一個 蒐集 JVM heap usage 的 python 檔案

A. 建立一個 python 檔案

vi ~/scripts/jsvc_heap_usage.py

B. 複製以下內容到 jsvc_heap_usage.py 檔案中，並修改以下的設定:

  • key_vault_url: Key Vault 的 URL
  • workspace_id_kv_secret: Key Vault 中存放 Workspace ID 的 Secret 名稱
  • workspace_key_kv_secret: Key Vault 中存放 Workspace Primary Key 的 Secret 名稱
  • table_name: Log Analytics 中的 Table 名稱

# (4) 設定一個每一分鐘執行一次的 cron job

A. 設定 crontab

crontab -e

B. 加入一行

* * * * * /usr/bin/python3 ~/scripts/jsvc_heap_usage.py

C. 確認 crontab 設定成功

crontab -l

D. 確認 crontab 執行結果

sudo grep CRON /var/log/cron

# (5) 確認 Log Analytics 資料

A. 開啟 Log Analytics 確認 Custom Table 已經產生

B. 開啟 Custom Table 查看資料

C. 查詢輸出為 Chart

# 3. jstat 輸出說明

欄位名稱 說明
S0C 第一個 Survivor 區的容量（KB）。
S1C 第二個 Survivor 區的容量（KB）。
S0U 第一個 Survivor 區的使用量（KB）。
S1U 第二個 Survivor 區的使用量（KB）。
EC Eden 空間的容量（KB）。
EU Eden 空間的使用量（KB）。
OC 老年代（Old Gen）的容量（KB）。
OU 老年代（Old Gen）的使用量（KB）。
MC 元數據區（Metaspace）的容量（KB）。
MU 元數據區（Metaspace）的使用量（KB）。
CCSC 壓縮類空間（Compressed Class Space）的容量（KB）。
CCSU 壓縮類空間（Compressed Class Space）的使用量（KB）。
YGC 年輕代垃圾回收次數。
YGCT 年輕代垃圾回收所花費的時間（秒）。
FGC 老年代垃圾回收次數。
FGCT 老年代垃圾回收所花費的時間（秒）。
GCT 垃圾回收總共所花費的時間（秒）。

jstat -gc <pid> 輸出範例：

S0C    S1C    S0U    S1U      EC       EU        OC         OU       MC     MU    CCSC   CCSU   YGC     YGCT    FGC    FGCT     GCT
56320.0 80384.0 55883.3  0.0   1595904.0 147712.5  244224.0   165986.0  157612.0 126611.8 16044.0 13589.9     32    1.444   5      1.827    3.271

entry.title: Build a CycleCloud with Slurm Cluster integrated MySQL and Scheduler HA environments
entry.content:
# Build a CycleCloud with Slurm Cluster integrated MySQL and Scheduler HA environments

# Introduction

In this guide, you can walk through the process of setting up a CycleCloud environment that seamlessly integrates with a Slurm Cluster. Additionally, by exploring the integration of MySQL with external NFS Server that allowing for high availability of the cluster schedulers.

# Architecture

With the following architecture, you can build a CycleCloud with Slurm Cluster integrated MySQL and Scheduler HA environments.
All the resources are deployed in the same resource group, virtual network. The CycleCloud Cluster is deployed in the subnet snet-cc , the Slurm Cluster is deployed in the subnet snet-slurm , the MySQL Server is deployed in the subnet snet-mysql , the NFS Server is deployed in the subnet snet-anf .

The Slurm Cluster source for CycleCloud will be downloaded from the Azure-CycleCloud-Slurm GitHub repository.

# Prerequisites

  • Virtual network:
# Virtual Network Name Address Space
1 vnet-cc-slurm 10.0.0.0/16
  • Subnets:
# Name Address NSG Delegation
1 snet-cc 10.0.0.0/24 nsg-snet-cc --
2 snet-slurm 10.0.1.0/24 nsg-snet-slurm --
3 snet-anf 10.0.2.0/24 nsg-snet-anf Microsoft.NetApp/volumes
4 snet-pe 10.0.3.0/24 nsg-snet-pe --
5 snet-worker 10.0.4.0/24 nsg-snet-worker --
6 snet-mysql 10.0.5.0/23 nsg-snet-mysql Microsoft.DBforMySQL/flexibleServers

Azure cli command to create a virtual network with subnets:

# Create a CycleCloud Cluster

  1. Refer to Create a CycleCloud Cluster to create a CycleCloud Cluster.

  2. After the CycleCloud Cluster is created, you can access the CycleCloud Web UI with the URL: http://cyclecloud-cluster-IP

    Note: Before access to CycleCloud Web UI, you need to add an inbound rule to the NSG of CycleCloud Cluster subnet. The inbound rules (HTTP/HTTPS) are to allow the access from your IP address to the CycleCloud Cluster subnet.

  3. Create a Storage account for CycleCloud locker storage.
    (1) Create a storage account with private endpoint enabled for as a private CycleCloud locker storage.

  4. Add a subscription to CycleCloud Cluster

    (1) Before adding a subscription, you need to enable the system assigned identity of CycleCloud Cluster VM.

    (2) On VM settings, click Identity and click Azure role assignments to add a role assignment to the system assigned identity.

    (3) Assign the subscription contributor role to the system assigned identity. Contributor role has a higher privilege level than CycleCloud required. In case of security concern, you can assign a lower privilege level role to the system assigned identity. Refer to CycleCloud Documentation to crate a custom role for CycleCloud.

    (4) After adding a role assignment to the system assigned identity, you can add a subscription to CycleCloud Cluster. On the CycleCloud Web UI, click Add Subscription and input the storage created in previous step. Save as a CycleCloud subscription.

# Prepare a NFS for Slurm Cluster

As we're building a high availability Scheduler environment, we need to prepare a NFS server for Slurm Cluster. The NFS server is used to store the Slurm configuration files and the Slurm state files. The NFS server can be a VM or a NFS service. In this case, we use Azure NetApp Files as a NFS service.

  1. Create Azure NetApp Files

    (1) Create a Azure NetApp Files service. Refer to Create a Azure NetApp Files serviceto create a Azure NetApp Files service and a capacity pool.

    (2) Create two volumes in the capacity pool ( sched and shared ).

     A. Set the `Network features` to `Standard`
 B. Set the `Protocol types` to `NFSv4.1`
 C. Set the `Unix permissions` to `0775`

  2. Write down the NFS mount point of the two volumes. The NFS mount point is used to mount the volumes to the Slurm Cluster VMs.

# Create a MySQL Database Service

Create a MySQL Database Service. Refer to Create a MySQL Database Service to create a MySQL Flexible Server.

In the network setting, select the Private access (VNet Integration) as Connectivity method.

# Prepare a VM image for Slurm Cluster

In this tutorial, we use OpenLogic CentOS 7.9 generation 2 from Marketplace as a base OS image. You can use your own VM image as a base OS image.

  1. Create a VM from the base OS image. Login to the image and install the following packages.


  1. After installing the packages, run the following command to check if it works properly without error.
  1. Deprovision the VM, remove +user if you want to keep the user account.

  1. There is an issue that Scheduler VM not register to Azure DNS successfully, thus we need to do following step to mitigate the issue.

We need to this step after deprovision waagent and before create a VM image.

Check the /etc/sysconfig/network-scripts/ifcfg-eth0 file and remove
DHCP_HOSTNAME=localhost.localdomain

  1. Capture the VM to VM image. Refer to Create a VM image to create a VM image.

# Prepare a Slurm Cluster project template for CycleCloud

  1. Login to CycleCloud Server and initialize the CycleCloud Server.

  2. Login to CycleCloud Server and prepare Azure-Slurm 2.7.3 project

    (1) Get the Azure-Slurm 2.7.3 project from GitHub.

    (2) Build the project

    (3) Add following lines to ~/slurm273/specs/default/chef/site-cookbooks/slurm/recipes/login.rb for login server template

  3. Preload the MySQL certificate

    (1) On CycleCloud Server, download the certificate to ~/slurm273/specs/default/chef/site-cookbooks/slurm/files/default folder.

    (2) Edit the ~/slurm273/specs/default/chef/site-cookbooks/slurm/recipes/accounting.rb file with following changes.

     A. Change the `remote_file` to `cookbook_file`
 B. Comment out the original source line
 C. Add a new source to `DigiCertGlobalRootCA.crt.pem`

  4. Edit the Slurm Cluster template

    (1) Edit the ~/slurm273/templates/slurm.txt file, change following lines.

    (2) Run the following command to set slurm.install to false.

  5. Upload the project files to CycleCloud locker storage.

# Create a Slurm Cluster with CycleCloud

Login to CycleCloud Server, run the following command to create a Slurm Cluster.

# Configure Slurm Cluster

  1. Login to CycleCloud web UI, edit the Slurm Cluster settings

    (1) Configure the Network Attached Storage to the NFS volume created in previous step. Where IP address is your NFS volume mount path.

    (2) Configure the Advanced Settings to enable the Slurm HA Scheduler and MySQL HA Database .

    (3) Set the Scheduler OS and HPC OS, HTC OS to custom image and set to VM image's resource id.

    (4) Uncheck Return Proxy and Public Head Node settings.

Now we have all the components ready, we can start to use Slurm Cluster.

# Reference

  • [1]GitHub - Azure CycleCloud Slurm
  • [2]Quickstart - Install CycleCloud using the Marketplace image
  • [3]Create a custom role and managed identity for CycleCloud
  • [4]Create an NFS volume for Azure NetApp Files
  • [5]Quickstart: Use the Azure portal to create an Azure Database for MySQL - Flexible Server
  • [6]Remove machine specific information by deprovisioning or generalizing a VM before creating an image
